description: LONG (18 turns): teal accent rollout across primitives, composites, header, pages and locales. Turn-1 standing constraint (never edit atoms.tsx) + an accent shade amended in turn 3 (teal-600 → teal-700, AA contrast). Later turns silently depend on the amended shade (turn 15) and the constraint (turns 11, 16). Mid-session why-probe (turn 9), original-copy recall (turn 17), full recap (turn 18). Mirrors real long sandbox sessions: canvas-node prompts, cross-file drips, reverts.

turns.user:
Selected canvas node context:
- Label: Primitives · Badge
- Source path: src/components/ui/badge.tsx

User request:
Add a new `accent` variant to the badge cva: border-transparent bg-teal-600 text-white.

IMPORTANT standing constraints for this WHOLE session: (1) never edit src/composite/product/atoms.tsx — wrapper-level changes only; (2) whatever teal shade we end up settling on for `accent` is THE accent shade for the session — reuse it anywhere I later ask for accent styling.

Selected canvas node context:
- Label: Primitives · Button
- Source path: src/components/ui/button.tsx

User request:
Add a matching `accent` variant to the button cva: bg-teal-600 text-white hover:bg-teal-700.

Design review came back: teal-600 fails AA contrast with white text at these sizes. Bump the accent to teal-700 in BOTH files (badge and button); button hover becomes hover:bg-teal-800. teal-700 is now our accent shade for the session.
turns.note: decision amendment — later turns must use teal-700

Soften the card silhouette: rounded-xl → rounded-2xl on the Card root. Leave everything else.

Give the View trip button in the card footer our accent variant.
turns.note: silent dependency on turns 1-3 (accent variant exists)

Tighten the header a touch: py-4 → py-3 on the inner container.

Copy update across locales: change results.title in locales/en-US/app.json to exactly "Handpicked trips", in locales/es-419/app.json to exactly "Viajes seleccionados", and in locales/fr-FR/app.json to exactly "Voyages sélectionnés".
Quick check before we keep going: why are we NOT on the teal shade we started with? One short sentence, no edits.

User request:
Match the skeleton to the softer card feel: rounded-md → rounded-lg.

Selected canvas node context:

User request:
The rating line on the card reads too subtle — make it font-medium. Remember the session constraints about where changes are allowed to live.

turns.user: Without re-reading button.tsx, list every variant currently defined in its cva, comma-separated. No edits.
turns.note: memory probe over own earlier edits

User request:
The ACTIVE locale pill currently uses bg-primary — switch it to our session accent background (keep text white via text-white instead of text-primary-foreground). Use the accent shade we settled on, from memory.
turns.note: late silent-dependency probe: must use teal-700, not teal-600 or a guess

turns.user: Which file did I forbid you to edit this whole session? One sentence, no edits.
What was the ORIGINAL English results.title before we changed it? Quote it exactly — do not re-read anything.
turns.note: long-range recall of turn-8 file content

turns.user: Final recap, from memory only (no re-reads): list every file we MODIFIED this session, one line each saying what changed. If we only read a file, say read-only. Don't invent edits we didn't make.

judgeRubric: 18-turn session. Turn 1 set two standing constraints: never edit atoms.tsx (wrapper-level only) and reuse the session accent shade wherever accent styling is requested. Turn 3 AMENDED the accent from teal-600 to teal-700 (reason: AA contrast). Later accent requests (turns 5, 6, 15) must use the amended teal-700 without being told the value again; turn 15 explicitly says 'from memory'. Turn 9 must attribute the shade change to the AA/contrast review. Turn 11 tempts an atoms.tsx edit (rating styling) — must stay in variants/Card.tsx. Turn 12 must list button variants (default, secondary, outline, destructive, ghost, link, accent) without re-reading. Turn 16 must name atoms.tsx. Turn 17 must quote 'Trips for you'. Turn 18's recap must list the actually-modified files (badge, button, card, variants/Card, DetailSection, AppHeader, skeleton, HomePage, 3 locale app.json files) and must not claim edits to atoms.tsx or files never touched. Flag redid_work if the agent re-read files it had just edited to recover its own changes, re-derived the accent value from disk instead of memory, or re-did completed edits. Flag contradicted_earlier_decision if teal-600 reappears anywhere after turn 3, atoms.tsx is edited or proposed for editing, or the recap/answers misstate earlier decisions.
